title: Using machine learning to predict future foster care admission.
ehr_evidence: We use claims data for children enrolled in a Medicaid health plan in Ohio as well as for linked adults, along with data on individual and geographic social determinants of health (SDOH) factors.
summary: A study using machine learning to predict foster care admission in Ohio children by analyzing Medicaid claims data and social determinants of health (SDOH).
key_findings: Gradient-boosted tree model identified 2408 children at risk of foster care admission.
SDOH data significantly improved predictive accuracy (PPV of 84.72% compared to 27.44% without SDOH).
population: children enrolled in a Medicaid health plan in Ohio
limitations: ethical and legal concerns regarding the use of such tools for selecting families for child welfare investigations
